Curaçao is the smallest nation to reach the World Cup, highlighting the expanded format’s inclusivity.

Article Title: Travis Kelce And Taylor Swift: "Set up 2 fake relationships": Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding talks intensify as the internet raises eyebrows over past claims
Article Text:
Taylor Swift–Travis Kelce wedding talks intensify as the internet raises eyebrows over past claims(Getty)

Old PR claims resurface as viral post puts focus on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce 's relationship

“In 2019, Travis Kelce’s publicist said he had previously set up 2 fake relationships to boost his clients’ fame.”

The Quicky

People

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding buzz grows as June date speculation draws attention



Us Weekly

The

Graham

Norton

Show

Wedding talk around Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce is picking up pace again. Reports hint at a possible date, and fans are tracking every move. But just as the excitement builds, a viral post gains traction.The post claims Kelce’s past links to PR-driven relationships, and that has sparked fresh debate online. While the couple remains private, the timing of these claims has led many to question the narrative around the Swift-Kelce romance.A viral Reddit post, which is now widely circulating, states:The claim traces back to Jack Ketsoyan, who appeared onpodcast in 2019.During that interview, Ketsoyan spoke about how PR relationships work in Hollywood. He explained that such arrangements are sometimes used to shift media focus or promote projects. He even shared an example in which a relationship was arranged to divert attention from a poorly reviewed film to an actor’s personal life.However, Ketsoyan did not mention Travis Kelce in that interview. The Reddit post links those comments to Kelce based on his current PR connections.Also, as per the article attached to the Reddit post, Kelce is represented by Full Scope Public Relations, which Ketsoyan co-founded with Pia Malihi in 2022. This connection is what has fueled speculation online about whether his relationship with Taylor Swift could be PR-driven.The discussion continued after a 2024 controversy. A document described as a breakup “media plan” involving Swift and Kelce surfaced online. Full Scope responded to, saying the documents were “entirely false and fabricated” and not authorised.Despite that denial, speculation continued in online spaces. An article attached to the post alluded that influencer QTCinderella claimed in a TikTok video that her make-up artist, who she said knew someone linked to Kelce’s publicist, described the relationship as PR-driven. “She said that it’s a PR relationship,” she claimed, adding that when she asked if it had turned real, the answer was “No.” There is no verified evidence supporting that claim.Another theory highlighted in the post involves Shamrock Capital. The comment points to its past connection to Swift’s masters, links to the NFL ecosystem, and the timing of the Eras Tour film deal with Disney+. They even highlight that the pop icon was able to repurchase all her masters after going public with Kelce. These points remain speculative and unverified.Even with the online chatter, focus remains on the wedding. According to, Swift and Kelce are reportedly eyeing June 13 in Rhode Island.The timing has raised questions. The Kansas City Chiefs have a mandatory minicamp scheduled from June 9 to 11, leaving a short window before the reported date.However, that timeline is not confirmed. A planner linked to the Ocean House venue publicly denied being involved in Swift’s wedding for that weekend. At the same time, Kylie Kelce said on her April 2 podcast that no details are being shared publicly.Swift herself had said earlier, onin Oct. 2025, that she had not started planning the wedding due to her music schedule.
Article Summary: Old PR claims resurface as viral post puts focus on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's relationship. The post claims Kelce’s past links to PR-driven relationships. While the couple remains private, the timing has led many to question the narrative around the Swift-Kelce romance.

Article Title: Nfl Trade Rumors: NFL Trade Rumors: Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets linked to national title-winning WR ahead of 2026 draft
Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumors: Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets linked to national title-winning WR ahead of 2026 draft (Getty)

NFL trade rumors : Pittsburgh Steelers , New York Jets eye different draft paths for Omar Cooper Jr. amid rising buzz



The 2026 draft buzz is picking up, and one name keeps popping up in trade rumors. Omar Cooper Jr. has quietly become one of the most talked-about wideouts ahead of April 2026. Both the Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets are now firmly in the mix.It makes sense, too. Teams want playmakers, and Omar Cooper rightfully fits that bill. After helping Indiana win a national title, his stock has only climbed. Now, the question is not if he goes early, but which team pulls the trigger first.The buzz intensified after a viral X post gained traction. The viral post by NFL rumors reads, “🚨🚨 The #Steelers and #The Jets are among the teams interested in Indiana wide receiver Omar Cooper Jr.”Also, if we look into other details, the link becomes clearer. Let’s start with the New York Jets. As reported by USA TODAY, the Jets hold four picks inside the top 44. That gives GM Darren Mougey serious flexibility. However, insider Tony Pauline noted that Cooper is mainly in play at No. 33 overall.That detail matters too. Most mock drafts project Cooper as a first-round talent. If he somehow slips into Round 2, the Jets could pounce.Pairing him with Garrett Wilson would instantly upgrade the offence. Cooper posted 69 catches, 937 yards, and 13 touchdowns last season. He brings a burst and red-zone threat, something the Jets need.Now shift to the Pittsburgh Steelers. Their interest feels more aggressive. According to Pro Football Focus mock draft released April 7, 2026, analyst Daire Carragher has Pittsburgh taking Cooper at No. 21.Carragher wrote in that mock that Cooper would “inject a spark into the Steelers’ offense” and highlighted his elite yards-after-catch ability. Cooper has also drawn a comparison to Deebo Samuel, who stands out. That comparison comes down to plat style. That tells you how teams view his skill set.Stat-wise, Cooper backs it up. Over his final two college seasons, he put up 97 catches, 1,531 yards, and 20 touchdowns. He also forced 27 missed tackles last year, ranking among the top receivers in college football.For Pittsburgh, the fit looks tempting. Adding him alongside DK Metcalf and Michael Pittman Jr. could create one of the league’s best receiver groups. The only concern remains quarterback stability.So here’s where it stands. The Jets may wait and react. The Steelers may strike early. Either way, this rising playmaker sits right in the middle of draft drama, and his landing spot could shape both offenses in 2026.
Article Summary: Omar Cooper Jr. has quietly become one of the most talked-about wideouts ahead of April 2026. Both the Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets are now firmly in the mix. Cooper posted 69 catches, 937 yards, and 13 touchdowns last season.

Article Title: Dez Bryant: Offset shooting update: Former Dallas Cowboys star Dez Bryant exposes alleged $8K debt after shooting chaos
Article Text:
Former Dallas Cowboys star Dez Bryant claimed Offset once owed him $8,000, sharing the story after the rapper’s reported casino shooting linked to a separate money dispute. While no direct connection is confirmed, Bryant’s comments have fueled speculation about a pattern, drawing fresh attention to Offset’s finances and recent controversy.

Dez Bryant’s claim adds fuel to Offset money controversy

Things have gotten a lot worse between Offset and the people around him, and this time it's not just in the music news. A shooting at a Florida casino has led to new questions, and money problems are quickly becoming part of the conversation. As speculation grows around a possible gambling-related conflict involving Lil Tjay, another unexpected voice has stepped in with a personal claim that adds even more intrigue.Former Dez Bryant did not hold back. The ex-Dallas Cowboys star stirred attention online after sharing his own story involving the rapper. His comments arrived at a time when fans were already trying to piece together what led to the altercation, making his claims hard to ignore.Bryant took to social media to recount a past encounter, offering a detailed and unfiltered version of events. He wrote, “I don’t ever talk about what I do or my relationships with anyone but I’m going to give yall a quick story between me and offset.”He then described the situation, saying, “I beat offset out of 8k he tried to take a nap on me and I go to tap on him to wake him up..telling him I need that 8k..”. The story did not end there.Bryant added, “his patnas saw me tapping on him..I can tell his patnas wanted to jump on some bullshit.. I chilled because I was by myself completely out numbered.. so I charged it to the game…”.What caught even more attention was the timing. Bryant revealed, “I swear to god Offset reached out to me yesterday 3 hours before he got shot on a money play… I thought about it and said..I’m good.. then I saw the news later on and was like damn…some people don’t play about they money.”He later reflected on the situation with a calmer tone, writing, “most importantly I’m not getting mad over nothing I can make within 5 minutes…. Some of us Carry a lot of sense…WTF I look like getting hurt over some money I can make 100 million times… kids don’t ever listen to a fool like this.”The comments have only deepened public curiosity. While no official link has been confirmed between Bryant’s story and the reported shooting, the overlap in themes has fueled online debate. It also shows how quickly personal problems can become public, especially when fame, money, and pride are involved.
Article Summary: Former Dallas Cowboys star Dez Bryant claimed Offset once owed him $8,000. His comments have fueled speculation about a pattern, drawing fresh attention to Offset's finances and recent controversy. No official link has been confirmed between Bryant’s story and the reported shooting, the overlap in themes has fueled online debate.

Article Title: "Another record in the works": Taylor Swift hints at new music as wedding plans with Travis Kelce take center stage
Article Text:
Taylor Swift is quietly working on a new album while focusing on her personal life with Travis Kelce, including ongoing wedding plans. Insiders say the project is still in early stages with no rush to release. She is also stepping back from touring, choosing to recharge and prioritize her relationship and future.

Taylor Swift keeps creative spark alive while prioritizing personal life with Travis Kelce

The attention on Taylor Swift isn't going away anytime soon, but it's clear that the focus has changed. After the excitement of her "The Life of a Showgirl" era, Swift is now trying to balance her creative goals with a very personal time in her life. With a high-profile relationship with Travis Kelce and rumors of a wedding growing, her next career move is happening more quietly than fans might think.Even though there is silence, that doesn't mean nothing is going on. Swift seems to be getting ready for what's next behind the scenes. While fans wait for news about her next album or tour, insiders say that this time she's being more careful and choosing patience over pressure as she deals with both love and her legacy.Multiple sources recently shed light on Swift’s current mindset, revealing that music remains very much part of her world, even if it is not ready for release. As one insider shared, “She does have another record in the works, but there’s no pressure to release it anytime soon. It’s still very much in the creative stages.”That approach reflects a noticeable shift. After years of relentless output and record-breaking tours, Swift is allowing herself space to breathe.Another source reinforced this idea, saying, “This was meant to be a lighter, fun chapter. She never meant to do a massive tour attached to it. Taylor’s priority right now is her personal life. She and Travis are saving all their energy for the big summer they have coming up. ”Swift herself has echoed that sentiment in past interviews. Speaking candidly about touring fatigue, she admitted, “No. I’m going to be honest with you, I’m so tired, like, when I think about doing it again. I want to do it really well, again.”That honesty offers insight into her current pace. Even as ideas continue to take shape, she is not rushing the process. A source previously noted, "always has ideas percolating," but is not "locked into" a new tour, adding "There have been ideas floated, but right now she is enjoying her break" and that they aren't "aware of any Showgirl tour plans. The Eras Tour was a once-in-a-lifetime experience, so she’s not going to put pressure on herself to make everything that big and ambitious. She’ll make sure the next one is special in its own right, but she’s also realistic about it."For now, Swift seems content writing her next chapter at her own speed, with Kelce by her side and creativity still quietly flowing.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift is quietly working on a new album while focusing on her personal life. Insiders say the project is still in early stages with no rush to release. She is also stepping back from touring, choosing to recharge and prioritize her relationship and future.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Panthers and Chargers deal gains traction with $14M WR at the center
Article Text:
The Los Angeles Chargers are projected to trade Quentin Johnston and the No. 22 pick to the Carolina Panthers for No. 19 and a fifth-rounder. With scheme changes and new receivers emerging, Johnston could become expendable. The move would help the Chargers gain draft flexibility while avoiding a costly fifth-year option decision.

Chargers weigh flexibility over patience in projected draft-day swap



The Los Angeles Chargers may be heading toward a quiet but meaningful roster shift, and it centers on a former first-round pick who has yet to fully cement his place. Trade chatter around Quentin Johnston is growing louder as draft season approaches, with league insiders pointing to both financial pressure and evolving team priorities. What once looked like a long-term investment now feels uncertain, especially as the front office reshapes the offense.A projected deal involving the Carolina Panthers has added fuel to that conversation. In this scenario, the Chargers would move Johnston along with the No. 22 pick to climb slightly higher in the draft. On the surface, it feels like a modest jump. Beneath that, it signals a team willing to pivot quickly rather than wait on potential.In a recent mock draft, Bill Barnwell outlined a deal where Los Angeles trades Johnston and the 22nd overall selection for pick No. 19 and an additional fifth-rounder. His reasoning reflects a deeper shift in how the Chargers may be building their offense moving forward:"The Chargers have said the right things about keeping Johnston, but the additions they made this offseason suggest that Los Angeles will be in 21 or 12 personnel more often than they were in 2025.Johnston would likely be the odd man out there, with Ladd McConkey as the lead wideout and Tre' Harris potentially running as the second wide receiver. The executives who drafted Johnston are no longer in the building in Los Angeles, and I'm not sure Joe Hortiz is as motivated to pick up Johnston's $18 million fifth-year option for 2027 as he would be for one of his own picks. "That context matters. Scheme changes often reshape depth charts faster than talent alone can. With Ladd McConkey emerging and Tre’ Harris pushing for snaps, Johnston’s role looks less secure than it did a year ago. Add in a looming May 1 deadline on his fifth-year option, and the timing of this rumor feels far from random.Still, this is not a simple sell-off. Johnston showed growth last season, posting 51 catches for 735 yards and eight touchdowns. That kind of production hints at untapped upside. The question is whether the Chargers believe that upside fits their new direction. If not, turning him into draft capital while moving up the board could be a calculated step forward rather than a step back.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Chargers are projected to trade Quentin Johnston and the No. 22 pick to the Carolina Panthers. The move would help the Chargers gain draft flexibility while avoiding a costly fifth-year option decision. With scheme changes and new receivers emerging, Johnston could become expendable.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Dallas Cowboys linked to Cleveland Browns’ No. 6 pick in bold draft-day scenario
Article Text:
The Dallas Cowboys are being urged to make an aggressive move in the 2026 NFL Draft by trading into the top 10. Insider Brian Broaddus believes targeting the Cleveland Browns’ No. 6 pick could secure an elite defensive talent. With two first-round picks, Dallas has flexibility, and a bold move could deliver an immediate impact player.

Brian Broaddus pushes Dallas toward a bold top-10 gamble

The Dallas Cowboys are staring at a rare opportunity, and the pressure to get it right is building fast. With two first-round picks in the upcoming 2026 NFL Draft, the front office has more flexibility than usual. That kind of capital does not come around often. It also invites bold thinking. Around the league, executives are watching closely to see if Jerry Jones and his team finally choose aggression over patience.Momentum is already shifting toward a decisive move. Internal conversations and outside voices are lining up around one idea. If the Cowboys truly want a difference-maker on defense, waiting at their current spots may not be enough. The buzz is no longer about who they might pick. It is about how far they are willing to go to get him.Longtime insider Brian Broaddus has added fuel to the conversation, pointing directly at the No. 6 pick currently held by the Cleveland Browns. His message was clear and urgent:"I think Cleveland is really, really anxious to get out of that spot at No. 6," Broaddus said. "I'd go ahead and try and set yourself up or make sure that you're in position to, if whoever one of those, whatever defensive player is there, if it's Bailey, Delane, whoever you want to take, just get in that spot and just wait it out.To me, that makes a lot of sense, especially to, you could probably grab a guy, one of these linebackers, the nickel corner, you could grab one of those guys at 39."I think that at 6, you're going to have your choice of a couple of defensive players, and you put yourself in maybe for an offensive player if you really want to do that too. The most important pick here is going to be the first one you make. That's going to be the guy that's probably going to be the biggest difference maker, at least this year. Go make sure you get the guy that you absolutely love. Don't sit there and wait at 12. "That urgency reflects a wider truth about Dallas. This roster is not rebuilding. It is trying to win now. A passive draft could waste another year of prime talent. On the other hand, a calculated trade up could land an elite defender who changes the tone of the entire unit.Names like David Bailey and Mansoor Delane are gaining traction for a reason. They offer immediate impact, not long-term hope. If Dallas believes one of them is the answer, standing still may be the bigger risk.
Article Summary: The Dallas Cowboys have two first-round picks in the 2026 NFL Draft. Insider Brian Broaddus believes targeting the Cleveland Browns’ No. 6 pick could secure an elite defensive talent. A passive draft could waste another year of prime talent. On the other hand, a calculated trade up could land an elite defender.

The situation escalated after viral rumors and legal debates surfaced. Meanwhile, Offset was shot near a Florida casino but is recovering. With Diggs also facing legal issues, the controversy has intensified.

Cardi B fears for her life as Offset shooting shocks amid Stefon Diggs drama

Tension around Cardi B and Offset has taken a darker turn, with both personal and public issues colliding at once. What began as online chatter tied to her relationship with Stefon Diggs has now spiraled into safety fears and a real-world incident that shocked fans. With legal questions, online harassment, and personal conflicts unfolding together, the spotlight on the trio has become harsher than ever.The controversy picked up pace when a viral screenshot, allegedly linked to Offset, triggered debate about the paternity of Cardi B’s newborn. The situation became more complex due to ongoing divorce proceedings and legal interpretations that quickly turned into gossip headlines. That online storm soon bled into something more troubling.Cardi B did not stay silent. She revealed the emotional toll in blunt terms, writing she was still “being harassed and threatened to the point that I feel like my life in danger” and warned that it is “all fun and games until it's too late.”In another moment of frustration, she called out the culture of viral attention, saying people were doing “anything for attention,” and that one “nasty upload could have serious real life consequences. ” Her words pointed to something deeper than celebrity drama. It showed how quickly digital narratives can spiral out of control.Meanwhile, Stefon Diggs has been dealing with his own legal troubles after filing a lawsuit against an influencer over serious allegations he has denied. That layer has only added to the noise surrounding his name.Then came the most alarming development. Offset was shot outside a casino in Florida, with police confirming two individuals were detained and an investigation is ongoing. Officials said the injuries were not life-threatening and the situation was quickly brought under control, but fans are still upset about what happened.The combination of legal problems, online harassment, and violence has made this more than just a celebrity headline. Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Buffalo Bills could “go all in for” $106M Las Vegas Raiders' defensive end amid record breaking season

Buffalo Bills could “go all in for” $106M Las Vegas Raiders' defensive end amid record breaking season.(Image via X)

Buffalo Bills might acquire the Las Vegas Raiders’ star player, Maxx Crosby, amid his contract drama



The Buffalo Bills have consistently performed well in the last few seasons but have lost their chance to reach the AFC championship after they lost brutally to the Seattle Seahawks in the Divisional round last season.As the team gears up to bounce back on the field, it seems they are working hard on the roster.A few hours ago, in a recent report, Garrett Podell of CBS Sports predicted that there is a chance that the Buffalo Bills could make a move to acquire the Las Vegas Raiders’ star player, Maxx Crosby, amid his contract drama with the Baltimore Ravens.Garrett Podell of CBS Sports noted in a recent report, “The pressure is on in Buffalo after the Bills failed to reach the AFC championship game in the 2025 season…”Garrett Podell of CBS Sports added, “General manager Brandon Beane executed a power play to roll Sean McDermott under the bus for this failure. He also traded a 2026 second-round pick to the Chicago Bears in exchange for wide receiver DJ Moore and a 2026 fifth-round pick.”Garrett Podell of CBS Sports continued, “It's time for all-in moves in Buffalo…Some salary cap maneuvering would certainly be required, but it's a move Beane could feel desperate to make.”However, the Buffalo Bills have stayed away from speculations concerning Maxx Crosby.Over the last two seasons, the Las Vegas Raiders’ defensive end, Maxx Crosby, has dominated the field with his consistently impressive performance even as he battled brutal injuries.In the last season, Maxx Crosby recorded 10 sacks, 73 tackles, two forced fumbles, and his first career interception in 15 games.This led to Maxx Crosby being the Las Vegas Raiders' winner for the 2025 Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year award along with being the highest paid non quarterback in the team.Back in 2024, Maxx Crosby recorded 7.5 sacks, 17 tackles for loss, and 20 quarterback hits across 12 games as his ankle injury forced him to skip 5 games.While Maxx Crosby has consistently broken records, his trade drama with the Baltimore Ravens seemed rather shocking as he reportedly failed his physical.For now, all eyes are on where Maxx Crosby ends up in the next season as rival teams continue to be linked to him.
Article Summary: Buffalo Bills might acquire the Las Vegas Raiders’ star player, Maxx Crosby, amid his contract drama with the Baltimore Ravens. Article Title: Drake Maye: Drake Maye’s New England Patriots strongly linked to $81M New York Giants nose tackle
Article Text:
Drake Maye’s New England Patriots strongly linked to $81M New York Giants nose tackle (Image via Getty: New York Giants logo and New England Patriots logo)

NFL Trade Rumors : Drake Maye ’s Patriots tied to Giants DT Dexter Lawrence

Trade Package:

New York could acquire Christian Barmore for losing Dexter Lawrence

New York HC John Harbaugh and GM Joe Schoen talk about Dexter Lawrence

New York Giants long-time nose tackle Dexter Lawrence signed a four-year, $87M contract extension in 2023, which included a $22M signing bonus, $60M in guaranteed money, and a $21M average annual salary. As per Spotrac, he is scheduled to make $18.5M base salary and a $500K workout bonus while carrying a $26.9M cap hit and a $13.9M dead cap value in 2026.In 2024, his contract was restructured by converting $10M base salary into a signing bonus. However, he has no guaranteed money left on the final two terms of the contract [2026 and 2027].As a result, a new contract was negotiated. But after several failed attempts at creating a new deal with New York, Lawrence has requested a trade, just two weeks before the draft. He will not be participating in the team’s upcoming voluntary offseason workouts.This allows analysts to involve him in a trade package for his future franchise. And so, Bleacher Report’s Gary Davenport suggests a trade with the Drake Maye-led New England Patriots. What does it contain? Let’s find out.New England receives Lawrence, whereas New York receives a 2026 second-round pick, a 2026 fourth-round pick, and a 2026 sixth-round pick in return for losing a valuable defensive lineman.The analyst notes that the Patriots have never shown they can be aggressive in defensive acquisitions, but if they could acquire the three-time Pro Bowler, they would form the league’s best defensive front alongside in-house Milton Williams and Christian Barmore.A page from their history? Last offseason, they signed several veterans: linebacker Robert Spillane, edge rusher Harold Landry III, defensive tackle Milton Williams, and cornerback Carlton Davis III. Result? They entered the Super Bowl.It looks like they are carrying forward the same approach this offseason. They have so far signed Chicago Bears safety Kevin Byard and Baltimore Ravens linebacker Dre’Mont Jones. This only shows how hungry they are for another Big Game appearance.If they wish to continue this pattern, they could easily bring in a proven interior defender on a new deal. This could make both parties content. Lawrence signs a new contract, and New England strengthens its defense.The analyst also suggests that the Giants could ask for Barmore in return, but the Patriots may want to continue with him.Barmore was facing domestic assault and battery charges stemming from an alleged Aug. 2025 altercation with his then-girlfriend. But the charges were dropped by the Massachusetts prosecutors just two days before the new league year [March 9]. They cited the case was “not viable for prosecution” due to the “current state of evidence” and the complainant’s reluctance to proceed.Anyhow, coming back to Giants tackle, how good was he on the gridiron? He had a down year in 2025 season, recorded career-low 31 tackles and half-sack. However, his PFF grades were great [except for run-defense grade].The defensive tackle earned a 75.6 overall PFF grade for the 2025 season, which ranked 9th among 134 qualified interior defensive linemen. His PFF pass-rush grade was 84.5, ranked 7th among others. His PFF run-defense grade was 57.0, ranking 56th. He recorded 34 total pressures, 27 hurries, 0 forced fumbles, and 7 hits.Giants head coach John Harbaugh spoke very highly about the defensive lineman in February: “How important is he? Really important. He’s super, super important. He’s a cornerstone football player, not really a cornerstone, more like the middle stone. He’s right in the middle. He’s a very big stone, and he’s a very active, athletic one.”If he is so important, then a contract could have been adjusted, but it wasn’t. Anyhow, New York general manager Joe Schoen did give everyone a hint about the same back at the NFL’s Annual Meeting in Phoenix. A reporter asked Schoen about the possibility of adjusting Lawrence’s contract.The GM said: “We have a lot of those conversations as we continue to build or open up money. Where can we do it? Could we do an extension with other players? What does that look like? So, we’re always having those conversations. Not only with Dexter, but with other players. That’s part of our mapping out the offseason and planning.”The planning probably reached no fruition. Or, in short, nothing in favor of the nose tackle. So, he’s requested a trade. And Bleacher Report’s Gary Davenport suggests the Drake Maye-led New England Patriots.As for the Patriots’ cap space, it is $35.5M, according to OverTheCap. Williams and Barmore occupy $45.6M of their $300M cap this year.Spotrac estimates Lawrence is likely to sign a three-year, $81M contract, resulting in a $27.2M average annual salary.So, would the New York All-Pro defensive tackle be traded to New England? Only time will tell…
Article Summary: Drake Maye’s New England Patriots strongly linked to $81M New York Giants nose tackle Dexter Lawrence. Lawrence signed a four-year, $87M contract extension in 2023, which included a $22M signing bonus, $60M in guaranteed money, and a $21M average annual salary.
